title: podman import, load, and commit are too verbose
body:
The progress should not be show for import, load, and commit.  It makes machine
parsing of the output much more difficult.  Also, each command should output an
image ID or name for the user.

Added a --verbose flag for users that still want to see progress.

Resolves issue #450

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body: I don't think we should add a verbose. Moving the output to stderr will allow tools that parse for stdout to work.  and will give the user what he wants which is some feedback on why something takes a long time.
I would prefer we continue to default to the output and add  a --quiet to turn the output off.

@rhatdan as Ed pointed out, it is a departure from docker to take this approach.  we discussed this in scrum today and verbose was the result.  my personal opinion is the output isnt all that helpful.  if you would prefer to take the quiet approach, will do so; please ack that decision.
If the output is not useful then we should just squelch it, and not add a new option, that no one will use.

My personal mark is if something can take longer than 3 seconds, then some sort of output should be shown to the user from time to time to give them a warm and fuzzy that stuff is happening.  If it were my final decision looking through the comments, I'd drop the verbose flag, write stuff to std.error except for the id at the end, and then let folks shuffle std.error to /dev/null if they don't want to see the verbose stuff.
